g.text: Only one task/operation at a time -> js in syncronous and single threaded.
we're able to achive async operations via-
1. execute the code if sync, push it into call stack, execute then pop
2. if there is any async code, setTimeout, setInterval, then start the timer and continue executing
3. setTimeout- when timer exipres the callback fn is pushed to TaskQueue (not executed)
4. event loop keeps on checking if the call stack is empty (when entire script is finished executing), when there is nothing to execute, we deque items and move to callStack and execute
1. execute the code if sync, push it into call stack, execute then pop
2. if there is any async code, setTimeout, then start the timer, or Promise do nothing and continue executing
3. setTimeout- when timer exipres the callback fn is pushed to TaskQueue (not executed)
4. Promises -> the success/failure these callbacks once resolved or rejected resp. queue to Microtask queue
5. Microtask queue, is having higher priority than Task Queue
6. Task queue is not going to run untill the Microtask Queue is empty. After each task in task queue, event loop will check
and run all tasks in microtask queue (if any).
7. event loop keeps on checking if the call stack is empty (when entire script is finished executing), athe script is done running
Promise - its a promise of future value. its an object having 3 states
